decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is currently at a moderate level (500 W/mK) but has room to grow towards its upper bound. Increasing conductivity by a factor of 1.5 (to 750 W/mK) will enhance lateral heat spreading, reducing the local hot spot temperature.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat distribution away from the chip center, directly lowering the peak temperature violation.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though this is a simulation.
